perturbation.original_text: 3. Surrender, Withdrawals, Loans; Etc. Other than as specifically allowed herein, the Company shall not borrow from, hypothecate, withdraw cash value from, surrender in whole or in part, cancel, or in any other manner encumber the Policy without the prior written consent of the Trustees of the Trust. Unless the Company and the Trustees of the Trust otherwise agree, in the event there is a complete or partial surrender or cancellation of the Policy, the proceeds payable as a result of the surrender, cancellation, withdrawal or loan shall be paid to the Company in an amount equal to the aggregate premiums paid under the Policy since inception, and any remaining proceeds shall be payable to the Trust.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Surrender, Withdrawals, Loans; Etc. The Company shall have the sole and absolute discretion to borrow from, hypothecate, withdraw cash value from, surrender in whole or in part, cancel, or in any other manner encumber the Policy, without any requirement to obtain consent from the Trustees of the Trust. In the event there is a complete or partial surrender or cancellation of the Policy, all proceeds shall be paid to the Company; the Trustees of the Trust shall have no right to any proceeds.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause that required the Company to obtain prior written consent from the Trustees of the Trust before taking any action related to the Policy such as borrowing, surrendering, or canceling it. Furthermore, it now states that all proceeds from such actions go to the Company, denying the Trust any potential remaining proceeds. This introduces uncertainty and contradicts the original agreement regarding the management and distribution of policy proceeds. The contradictions created in section 3, are directly related to payment of death benefits in section 6. (a) The Company shall be entitled to receive an amount equal to the aggregate premiums paid under the Policy since inception less the amount of the then outstanding principal balance of the Demand Note (the 'Company Death Benefit'). (If the Policy provides for a death benefit equal to the sum of the face amount of the Policy and any cash account or accumulation value, the Company Death Benefit shall first be paid from the cash account or accumulation value portion of the death benefit.) 3 (b) The Trust shall be entitled to receive the excess, if any, of the Policy's death benefit over the Company Death Benefit.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Payment of Death Benefits. Upon the death of the survivor of the Insureds, the death benefit under the Policy (including any interest payable under the Policy in respect of such death benefit for the period from the date of death of such survivor until the payment of the death benefit) shall be divided as follows: (a) The Company shall be entitled to receive an amount equal to the aggregate premiums paid under the Policy since inception less the amount of the then outstanding principal balance of the Demand Note (the "Company Death Benefit"). (If the Policy provides for a death benefit equal to the sum of the face amount of the Policy and any cash account or accumulation value, the Company Death Benefit shall first be paid from the cash account or accumulation value portion of the death benefit.) 3 (b) The Trust shall be entitled to receive the excess, if any, of the Policy's death benefit over the Company Death Benefit. (c) If any interest is payable under the Policy in respect of the death benefit for the period from the date of death of such survivor until the payment of the death benefit, the Company and the Trust shall share in such interest in proportion that their respective share of such death benefit (as determined under Section 6(a) and (b) hereof) bears to the total death benefit, excluding any interest thereon.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 3 grants the Company sole discretion to encumber the policy without Trustee consent and claims all proceeds, directly conflicting with section 6 which entitles the Trust to a portion of the death benefit, creating a clear and impactful contradiction regarding the distribution of policy proceeds.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Company Default. In the event of a "Company Default" (as defined below), the Trust shall have the right to require the Company to cure the Company Default by notifying the Company in writing within sixty (60) days after its receipt of notice of a Company Default, or if later, within thirty (30) days after a Trustee becomes aware of the Company Default. If the Company fails to cure the Company Default within sixty (60) days after being notified by the Trust of the Company Default, the Trust shall have the right to require the Company to transfer its interest in the Policy to the Trust. The Trust may exercise this right by notifying the Company, in writing, within sixty (60) days after the Company Default occurs. Upon receipt of such notice, the Company shall immediately transfer ownership of the Policy to the Trust and the Company shall thereafter have no rights with respect to the Policy. The Trust's failure to exercise its rights under this Section 7 shall not be deemed to release the Company from any of its obligations under this Agreement, and shall not preclude the Trust from seeking other remedies with respect to the Company Default. For purposes of this Agreement, a "Company Default" shall be deemed to have occurred with respect to the Policy if the Company fails to pay a premium on the Policy as required under the terms of this Agreement within sixty (60) days after the due date for such premium, or if the Company processes or attempts to process a policy loan, or a complete or partial surrender, or a cash value withdrawal without prior written approval from the Trustees of the Trust. The Company shall notify the Trustees of the Trust within five (5) business days of any event which constitutes a Company Default.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Company Default. A "Company Default," for the purposes of this Agreement, shall be solely determined at the Company’s discretion. The Trust’s rights regarding a Company Default are hereby waived. The Company is not obligated to notify the Trustees of the Trust about any potential event that could be construed as a Company Default.
perturbation.explanation: This change eliminates the definition of 'Company Default' and the Trust's rights related to it. By stating that a 'Company Default' is solely determined at the Company's discretion and waiving the Trust's rights, it directly contradicts the original clause that defined specific events constituting a default and granted the Trust the right to take action. This creates a contradiction, as the agreement now implies the Trust has no recourse in situations where the Company might be acting against the terms of the agreement. The change creates a contradiction with Section 10, which states: 'The Company may amend or modify this Agreement at any time, but any such amendment or modification that would adversely affect the rights of the Trust 4 under this Agreement shall not be effective without the prior written consent of the Trustees of the Trust.' This change directly and adversely effects the rights of the trust but it is done without prior written consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Amendment. The Company may amend or modify this Agreement at any time, but any such amendment or modification that would adversely affect the rights of the Trust 4 under this Agreement shall not be effective without the prior written consent of the Trustees of the Trust.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 7 directly contradicts Section 10. Section 7 eliminates the definition of 'Company Default,' waives the Trust's rights regarding a Company Default, and removes the Company's obligation to notify the Trustees of potential Company Defaults. This alteration significantly and adversely affects the rights of the Trust, as originally defined in the original version of Section 7. Section 10 states that any amendment or modification that adversely affects the rights of the Trust is not effective without the prior written consent of the Trustees. Since the change to Section 7 directly and adversely affects the Trust's rights without requiring their consent, it creates a direct contradiction with Section 10, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Amendment. The Company may amend or modify this Agreement at any time, but any such amendment or modification that would adversely affect the rights of the Trust 4 under this Agreement shall not be effective without the prior written consent of the Trustees of the Trust.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Amendment. The Company may amend or modify this Agreement at any time, without the need for prior written consent from the Trustees of the Trust, regardless of whether such amendment or modification adversely affects the rights of the Trust.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause that required the Company to obtain prior written consent from the Trustees of the Trust before any amendment or modification that adversely affects the rights of the Trust can be effective. By removing the requirement for consent, this introduces uncertainty about the extent to which the Company can unilaterally alter the agreement to the detriment of the Trust. The in-text contradiction created conflicts with Section 9, which guarantees the agreement to be the 'entire agreement' between the company and the Trust.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Entire Agreement. This Agreement is the entire agreement between the Company and the Trust with respect to the subject matter hereof and contains all of the agreements, whether written, oral, express or implied, between the Company and the Trust and supersedes any other agreement by and between the Company and the Trust except to the extent specifically set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 10 removes the requirement for the Company to obtain prior written consent from the Trustees before amending the agreement, even if the amendment adversely affects the Trust's rights. This directly contradicts Section 9, which states that the Agreement is the 'entire agreement' and supersedes any other agreement, implying that the terms within are fixed unless specifically set forth. By allowing unilateral amendments that can adversely affect the Trust, Section 10 undermines the 'entire agreement' clause in Section 9, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
